op_label: Op 52 (Wave 2 — pairing bridge + dark-launch audit round)
supersedes_note: Op 52 delivered: (a) TGP re-scoped mobile-native, extension is desktop-only capture bridge; (b) DESIGN v0.3 landed on PR #2 (mobile-app-initiated 6-digit pairing flow, retiring inline password); (c) R3-CLARIFY-1 ruling landed in context repo (word-boundary anchored token scan replacing raw substring scan); (d) three backend PRs opened (#500 progress+complete, #501 scout/ingest, #502 pair endpoints); (e) full R72 dual-lens Wave 2 audits complete on all 5 open PRs; (f) both extension PRs fully patched to CLEAN status pending re-audit; (g) three backend PRs require operator ruling on cross-cutting dark-route guard-ordering finding before fixer round.

headline_status.current_op: Op 52 — Wave 2 audits complete; awaiting operator ruling on dark-route ordering finding; backend fixer round pre-staged
headline_status.blocked_on: Operator ruling: cross-cutting P2 on all 3 backend PRs — controller-scoped feature-flag guards run AFTER global JwtAuth/Roles guards, so flag-off routes leak existence via 401/403 instead of 404. Independently confirmed by both Lens A and Lens B on PR #500, both lenses on PR #501, and Lens A on PR #502. Options: (A) fix repo-wide by moving feature-flag guards to a middleware layer that runs before auth, or (B) waive as acceptable dark-launch behavior and downgrade the guard's self-documented 'ships dark' claim to '404 only for authenticated coach role, 401/403 otherwise'.
headline_status.next_human_decision: Rule on dark-route ordering finding (option A vs B above).

immutable_doctrine_in_force.R3_authorship: Every commit shows Bradley Gleave <bradley@bradleytgpcoaching.com> as author AND committer.
immutable_doctrine_in_force.R3_CLARIFY_1_2026_07_06: R3 forbidden-token scan uses word-boundary anchored patterns only. See roadmap/rulings/R3-CLARIFY-1_2026-07-06.md. Canonical patterns: \bAI\b (case-sensitive); \b(agent|assistant|computer)\b (case-insensitive); \b(claude|opus|sonnet|haiku|gpt|copilot|gemini|llm|chatgpt|anthropic)\b; co-authored-by:; generated-by:; \bai-(generated|assisted|written|authored)\b. Substrings like 'chai', 'fail', 'aix', 'maintain', 'domain' inside English words are NOT violations.
immutable_doctrine_in_force.R_META_4_forbidden_models: Only claude_opus_4_8 (fixer/builder) + gpt_5_5 (auditor) may commit or audit. Sonnet / Haiku / Gemini forbidden.
immutable_doctrine_in_force.R11_lens_isolation: Lens A and Lens B run in /tmp/*_lens_{a,b}/ with independent clones. No cross-talk.
immutable_doctrine_in_force.R14_R15_no_merge_without_clean: No PR merges until CLEAN of P0-P3.
immutable_doctrine_in_force.R72_dual_lens: All P0-P3 PRs require parallel Opus 4.8 + GPT-5.5 adversarial review before merge.
immutable_doctrine_in_force.R80_envelope_source_of_truth: extractors/_interface.js in tgp-importer-extension is the entity envelope source of truth. Backend DTOs MUST match.
immutable_doctrine_in_force.R124_sha_verify_both_ways: gh api head.sha MUST match local git rev-parse HEAD before merge.

product_pivot_this_session.confirmed_2026_07_06: TGP is a MOBILE APP. Desktop extension cannot share cookies/origin with the app. Bridge is a 6-digit pairing code.
product_pivot_this_session.site_agnostic_vision: JSON captured as it loads on any site → routed to TGP DB → popup lives in browser always → auto-installed with TGP main app (deferred) → popup has TGP sign-in via 6-digit code → data goes to that account. No per-platform tooling.
product_pivot_this_session.auto_install_cta: Deferred. In-app 'Install extension' CTA is future work.
flow_landed_in_DESIGN_v0_3: 1. User taps 'Import my data' in TGP mobile
6. If browser already signed into TGP: auto-login (deferred v0.3.5)
7. Otherwise: pairing code shown on mobile
9. Extension calls /pair/redeem → gets coach-bound Supabase session
11. Extension hops pages, captures JSON → posts to /api/scout/ingest

open_prs.head: 3506584b95b89197b7dbfe12a7a252bec266f7f8
open_prs.title: feat(scout): POST /api/scout/progress + /api/scout/ingest/complete — cross-device progress + completion (FEATURE_SCOUT_INGEST off) [IMPORTER-E] [LOC-EXEMPT: test coverage + RLS migration + prisma schema; shipped src is small]
open_prs.wave_2_status: AUDITED — FAIL. Requires backend fixer round.

wave_2_findings_blocking.detail: @Controller('api/scout') double-prefixes with global setGlobalPrefix('api') → routes mount at /api/api/scout/* not /api/scout/*. scout.controller.spec.ts:134 enshrines the wrong path. Extension would 404 on every call.
Controller-scoped ScoutFeatureFlagGuard runs AFTER global JwtAuthGuard/RolesGuard, so flag-off leaks existence via 401/403 instead of 404. CROSS-CUTTING — hits #500, #501, #502.
final_counts has no size/shape cap (only @IsObject() + ~100kB body limit).
Completion notification/analytics at-most-once — crash between commit and push loses them permanently. Needs outbox or documented waiver.
In-process 5s coalescing cache; pre-completion flush only drains local pod.

open_prs.head: 14692496c12798df57bbd8b51b693c030468cdb9
open_prs.title: feat(scout): POST /api/scout/ingest — extension crawl envelope receiver (FEATURE_SCOUT_INGEST off) [IMPORTER-B] [LOC-EXEMPT: 846 net — 239 prod src under the 400 cap; overage is R74-mandated test LOC (541) + migration/schema SQL]
open_prs.wave_2_status: AUDITED — FAIL. Requires backend fixer round.

wave_2_findings_blocking.detail: extractors/_interface.js is internally inconsistent: comment says {source_id, payload} with provenance inside payload, but makeEntity() emits {sourceId (camelCase), sourcePlatform, payload, capturedAt} with provenance at top level. DTO/service implement the comment, not the code. Verbatim makeEntity() batches 400. Flagged in PR body but unresolved. Needs DTO alignment OR operator ruling amending contract.
Same cross-cutting finding as #500.

Service stores entity.payload verbatim JSONB — redaction fully delegated to extension. Regression there = secrets at rest. Fix: server-side sensitive-key denylist strip, OR operator waiver assigning redaction to extension only.
JwtAuthGuard/RolesGuard are already global APP_GUARDs but re-declared in @UseGuards and re-provided locally → execute twice per request.
ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING replay safety + in-batch dup collapse + RLS deny-all asserted only in comments/DDL, not integration tests.

open_prs.head: 2a01ac826a6d3f3ff2902b781dd0faa3b3cdad12
open_prs.title: feat(extension): pairing-code endpoints — init + status + redeem (FEATURE_EXTENSION_PAIRING off) [IMPORTER-D]
open_prs.wave_2_status: AUDITED — FAIL. Most work. Requires backend fixer round.

wave_2_findings_blocking.detail: init and status handlers lack @Roles('coach'); CoachGuard alone doesn't satisfy roles-enforced.spec.ts meta-test. CI RED.
test:src density 1.87 < 2.0 (below R74 floor), no [TEST-EXEMPT] in title.
Net +7 @ts-expect-error casts without required #NNNN ticket refs.
R100.A3 combined LOC 1367 > 400; no [LOC-EXEMPT]. Note: R76 prod-only net (398) PASSES; only the broader combined gate fails.
No per-code failed-attempt cap, no global redeem budget. Only per-IP throttle (botnet-bypassable). Fix: implement 5-attempt cap + permanent burn per DESIGN v0.3.
Raw 6-digit pairing code (a live credential) travels in GET query string on /status → lands in proxy/LB/browser access logs. Fix: move to header or opaque pairing_id per DESIGN v0.3 P2 fix.
/init has no per-coach flood-mint throttle. Impact limited (attacker floods own coach_id → storage DoS only).
redeem's timingSafeStrEqual on 6-digit strings is effectively a no-op given trivial input space.
Feature-flag guard 404 message is POST-specific but applies to GET /status too.
No explicit service_role RLS policy (relies on BYPASSRLS). Matches precedent but worth explicit policy for clarity.

open_prs.wave_2_verified_pass_notes: R3 identity + anchored token scan clean; R80 chosen_platform binding correct; symmetric down.sql; RLS FORCE + RESTRICTIVE deny-all + service_role bypass; indexes on code/expires_at/used_at; CSPRNG codes; TTL clamped [30,300] server-side; feature flag off → 404 (verified for POST /init and /redeem, guard-message P3 aside); atomic single-statement CAS on used_at; no existence leak on unknown/foreign codes; no raw 6-digit code in any log.

cross_cutting_issue_needing_operator_rul.detail: Global APP_GUARDs (JwtAuthGuard, RolesGuard, UserThrottlerGuard) execute BEFORE the controller-level FeatureFlagGuard. With flag OFF: unauth → 401, non-coach → 403, coach → 404. The guard's self-doc says 'indistinguishable from an unmounted route' which is false unless the caller is already the intended coach role.
cross_cutting_issue_needing_operator_rul.option_A_fix: Move feature-flag guards to a global middleware or an ExceptionFilter that intercepts before auth, converting any request against a disabled route to 404 regardless of auth status. Repo-wide fix. ~50 net LOC + tests.
cross_cutting_issue_needing_operator_rul.option_B_waive: Downgrade the 'ships dark' contract to 'coach role gets 404, others get standard auth errors'. Amend guard docstrings + DESIGN. Zero code change.
cross_cutting_issue_needing_operator_rul.recommendation: Option A is more defensible against enumeration attacks but adds complexity. Option B is pragmatic — an attacker learning 'this backend has ANY /api/scout endpoint' is low-value once the extension is public knowledge.

context_repo_changes_this_session.rationale: User: 'the fact that any word containing ai is flagged is insanioty - but why does ai need to be banned anyways?' Ruling clarifies R3 forbidden-token scan uses word-boundary anchored patterns, not raw substrings. Canonical regex set defined.

next_actions_ordered: Get operator ruling on DARK_ROUTE_GUARD_ORDERING cross-cutting P2 (option A repo-wide fix, or option B waiver).
Dispatch backend fixer round on PRs #500, #501, #502 in parallel (claude_opus_4_8, 3 subagents). Scope files pre-staged; use the wave-2 audit reports as canonical scope.

For PR #501 R80 envelope-mismatch P1: needs operator ruling OR the fixer resolves by aligning extension _interface.js makeEntity() to match the DTO. Whichever is authoritative dictates the fix direction.
After backend fixers land, dispatch fresh Lens A + Lens B on all 3 backend PRs (gpt_5_5, 6 auditors).
Also dispatch fresh Lens A + Lens B on extension PRs #1 and #2 (re-audit after wave-2 fixes).
Merge order once ALL CLEAN of P0-P3: #2 (docs, no deps) → #1 (C1 extension foundation) → #501 (scout/ingest, then #500 progress+complete which shares FEATURE_SCOUT_INGEST) → #502 (pair endpoints).
R5-archive round-2 audits to audits/PR{N}-LENS-{A,B}-LIVE.<sha8>.archive.md in context repo.
Update THIS current-state.json + HANDOFF_WAVE2.md after each merge.
After all 5 PRs merged: hand Chrome-loadable v0.3 build to operator; write handoffs/importer-wave/postmortem.md per R137.
gotchas_new_this_session: gh pr edit body fails silently on repos with project associations — use gh api -X PATCH repos/.../pulls/N --input <(echo body_json) instead.
R3-CLARIFY-1: NEVER trigger on English substrings like 'chai', 'fail', 'aix', 'maintain', 'domain'. Anchored patterns only.

Subagents of type 'codebase' cannot be messaged mid-run (message_subagent rejects with 'cannot be interrupted'). Wave-1 wasted ~10 minutes learning this. Preload the full scope up front and let them run to completion.
/tmp/claude_code_output.md is a shared filename across all codebase subagents — each subagent will clobber it. Persist to lane-specific paths.
Session sandbox pauses across day boundaries — /tmp survives, subagent state survives, git worktrees survive. Verified 2026-07-06 → 2026-07-07 gap: builders finished cleanly during the pause.
verdict: IN_PROGRESS — awaiting operator ruling on dark-route ordering, then dispatch backend fixer round.
